programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP10: JOE ARIBO
programme.sub-title: Despite finishing as runners-up with Glasgow Rangers in the Europa League as well as scoring in the final, Joe Aribo decided his journey would continue away from Scotland, instead opting for the Premier League with Southampton. This week on The Big Interview, Aribo reflects on his Nigerian roots, growing up playing football in the cages of South-East London, how he studied sports science and how he’s finding life on the south coast.
programme.desc: Despite finishing as runners-up with Glasgow Rangers in the Europa League as well as scoring in the final, Joe Aribo decided his journey would continue away from Scotland, instead opting for the Premier League with Southampton. This week on The Big Interview, Aribo reflects on his Nigerian roots, growing up playing football in the cages of South-East London, how he studied sports science and how he’s finding life on the south coast.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP20: (Subbed) EMMANUEL ADEBAYOR
programme.sub-title: PL Stories looks into the extraordinary journey of Emmanuel Adebayor, from his prolific Premier League career with Arsenal, Manchester City, Tottenham, and Crystal Palace, to the harrowing experience of surviving the Togo bus attack in 2010. We dive deep into his rise as one of Africa's finest football talents, and how the near-death tragedy profoundly impacted his life, shaping his resilience and outlook beyond the game.
programme.desc: PL Stories looks into the extraordinary journey of Emmanuel Adebayor, from his prolific Premier League career with Arsenal, Manchester City, Tottenham, and Crystal Palace, to the harrowing experience of surviving the Togo bus attack in 2010. We dive deep into his rise as one of Africa's finest football talents, and how the near-death tragedy profoundly impacted his life, shaping his resilience and outlook beyond the game.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP43: (Subbed) FOXES FANTASY WORLD
programme.sub-title: After watching their already relegated side lose to the Invincibles on the last day of the 2003/4 Premier League season, some Leicester City fans stayed behind to watch Arsenal lift the trophy at Highbury. Those fans endured ten years in the wilderness, only for Leicester to return to the big time and embark on a rollercoaster couple of years. In that time they completed the great escape after being dead and buried at the foot of the table and then followed that up with the most unthinkable of Premier League triumphs. This is their story, one of sports greatest triumphs, told by those who wrote it.
programme.desc: After watching their already relegated side lose to the Invincibles on the last day of the 2003/4 Premier League season, some Leicester City fans stayed behind to watch Arsenal lift the trophy at Highbury. Those fans endured ten years in the wilderness, only for Leicester to return to the big time and embark on a rollercoaster couple of years. In that time they completed the great escape after being dead and buried at the foot of the table and then followed that up with the most unthinkable of Premier League triumphs. This is their story, one of sports greatest triumphs, told by those who wrote it.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP11: JAMES JUSTIN
programme.sub-title: Born and raised a Luton Town fan, James Justin rejected the chance to join Arsenal as a youngster to play for his childhood team. The Big Interview sits down with the Leicester defender to talk about his love for his hometown club, how the loss of his father inspires him to greater things, the role Enzo Maresca played in his development and how he’s looking ahead to Leicester’s big game against rivals Nottingham Forest.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP18: DYLAN TOMBIDES
programme.sub-title: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. Despite a courageous three-year battle, he tragically succumbed to the illness at the age of 20 in 2014. We look back on the life of Dylan told through those who knew and loved him best, and how 10 years on since his unfortunate passing his memory still lives on through the DT38 foundation.
programme.desc: He was a star of Australia's U23 and U17 teams and made his West Ham debut at just 18 years of age, but in 2011 Dylan Tombides life was turned upside down when he received a diagnosis of testicular cancer following a routine drug test during the Under-17 FIFA World Cup. Despite a courageous three-year battle, he tragically succumbed to the illness at the age of 20 in 2014. We look back on the life of Dylan told through those who knew and loved him best, and how 10 years on since his unfortunate passing his memory still lives on through the DT38 foundation.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP07: ANDY ROBERTSON
programme.sub-title: Andy Robertson has been integral to the Liverpool team which has won everything in recent years, from the Premier League to the UEFA Champions League. However, his route to the top hasn’t always been straight-forward. Robertson opens up about being released by his boyhood club Celtic, playing and working for Queens Park, Jackie McNamara’s importance to him whilst at Dundee and how he’s adapting to life under new Liverpool boss, Arne Slot.
programme.desc: Andy Robertson has been integral to the Liverpool team which has won everything in recent years, from the Premier League to the UEFA Champions League. However, his route to the top hasn’t always been straight-forward. Robertson opens up about being released by his boyhood club Celtic, playing and working for Queens Park, Jackie McNamara’s importance to him whilst at Dundee and how he’s adapting to life under new Liverpool boss, Arne Slot.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP11: JAMES JUSTIN
programme.sub-title: Born and raised a Luton Town fan, James Justin rejected the chance to join Arsenal as a youngster to play for his childhood team. The Big Interview sits down with the Leicester defender to talk about his love for his hometown club, how the loss of his father inspires him to greater things, the role Enzo Maresca played in his development and how he’s looking ahead to Leicester’s big game against rivals Nottingham Forest.
programme.desc: Born and raised a Luton Town fan, James Justin rejected the chance to join Arsenal as a youngster to play for his childhood team. The Big Interview sits down with the Leicester defender to talk about his love for his hometown club, how the loss of his father inspires him to greater things, the role Enzo Maresca played in his development and how he’s looking ahead to Leicester’s big game against rivals Nottingham Forest.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP21: MARTIN O’NEILL
programme.sub-title: Before stepping into the dugout, Martin O’Neill was part of one of football’s most iconic teams with Brian Clough’s Nottingham Forest. Hear from Martin as he shares stories from his remarkable journey across nearly five decades in the game. From European heartbreak, to promotional triumph, discover what shaped his extraordinary career, both on the field and from the sidelines.
programme.desc: Before stepping into the dugout, Martin O’Neill was part of one of football’s most iconic teams with Brian Clough’s Nottingham Forest. Hear from Martin as he shares stories from his remarkable journey across nearly five decades in the game. From European heartbreak, to promotional triumph, discover what shaped his extraordinary career, both on the field and from the sidelines.
